stories.title: Open Championship Eve — What to Watch as the 154th Major Begins Thursday at Royal Birkdale

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The 154th Open Championship begins TOMORROW at Royal Birkdale.

Scheffler: +700
McIlroy: +850
Fleetwood: +1500
Fitzpatrick: +1500

First groups tee off ~1:30 AM ET Thursday. Set your alarms. ⛳

#TheOpen #OpenChampionship

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Royal Birkdale. 7,223 yards. Par 70. Links golf at its most demanding.

Scheffler defends. McIlroy hunts No. 7. Fleetwood plays his hometown major.

This is the week golf fans wait for all year. Who's your pick?

#TheOpen

stories.title: McIlroy Chasing History — Major No. 7 Would Break European Record Outright

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Rory McIlroy has 6 major titles.

A 7th at Royal Birkdale would make him the European man with the most major victories in history — breaking the record outright.

He hasn't won The Open since 2014. He is playing some of the best golf of his life.

#TheOpen #RoryMcIlroy

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Back-to-back Masters titles. 6 career majors. +850 at Royal Birkdale.

If Rory McIlroy wins The Open this week, he writes himself into a chapter of golf history that nobody else has reached.

Major No. 7. Most ever by a European man.

#TheOpen #RoryMcIlroy

article.headline: Rory McIlroy Is Chasing Major No. 7 at Royal Birkdale — and the Historical Stakes Could Not Be Higher

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
McIlroy Major No. 7

#### Image Concept 1 — Social (1080x1350)
- **Search term:** Rory McIlroy
- **Usage:** Morning X/FB post
- **Caption overlay:** "CHASING MAJOR No. 7" / "European Record on the Line"

#### Image Concept 2 — Article Hero (1200x630)
- **Search term:** Rory McIlroy
- **Usage:** Article featured image

---

research.facts:
McIlroy Major No. 7
**Key verified facts:**
- McIlroy has 6 major titles entering the 154th Open (MEDIUM — research)
- 7 majors would tie/break European record: currently tied with Nick Faldo, Lee Trevino, Phil Mickelson at 6 (MEDIUM — research notes Faldo comparison)
- McIlroy won back-to-back Masters: 2025 and 2026 (MEDIUM)
- 2026 Masters won April 12, held off Scheffler by 1 shot, final-round 71, 12-under 276 total (MEDIUM)
- Last won The Open in 2014 (HIGH — historical record)
- First back-to-back Masters champion since Tiger Woods 2001-02 (MEDIUM)
- Completed Career Grand Slam with his first Masters win (2025) (MEDIUM)
- McIlroy finished T7 at Scottish Open at -12 (MEDIUM — pipeline notes July 13)
- McIlroy odds: +850 (MEDIUM)
**Flags:**
- "Break European record outright" — the research says "most European men's majors ever" if he wins No. 7. The research mentioned tying Faldo's record. Need to be accurate: 7 would give him MORE than Faldo (6). Phrase carefully: "most major victories by a European man" — verify against research. Research says "tie Nick Faldo's record as the European with the most men's major championships" if McIlroy wins... which means Faldo also has 6. A 7th would BREAK the record, not tie it. Use "break" not "tie."
- Trevino and Mickelson are not European, so be careful — the "European" framing is about being European. Trevino (American) and Mickelson (American) are separate. The European record is specifically about European-born players. Faldo has 6. So 7 would be outright most by a European man. This is correct per research.

stories.title: Fleetwood's Moment — Hometown Star Takes On The Open at the Course Next Door

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Tommy Fleetwood grew up in Southport.

He is playing The Open this week at Royal Birkdale — which is also in Southport.

His dad is on the bag. No English player has won The Open since 1992.

This week means everything to him.

#TheOpen #TommyFleetwood

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
World No. 3. Best player without a major. Hometown course.

Tommy Fleetwood is the sentimental pick of the week for a reason.

Can he do it at Birkdale? #TheOpen #TommyFleetwood

stories.title: Haeran Ryu's Historic Evian — The Round of 60 and a Playoff That Defined Her Summer

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Haeran Ryu shot a 60 in Round 3 of the Evian Championship.

Lowest score ever in an LPGA major championship.

Then she won in a playoff.

Back-to-back LPGA majors. What a summer she is having. 🏌️‍♀️

#LPGA #EvianChampionship

article.headline: Haeran Ryu Shot a 60 in a Major. Then She Won in a Playoff. South Korea's Star Has Redefined the LPGA Summer.

research.facts:
Haeran Ryu / Evian
**Key verified facts:**
- Evian Championship (Amundi Evian Championship) July 9-12, 2026 (HIGH)
- Venue: Evian Resort Golf Club, Evian-les-Bains, France (HIGH)
- Winner: Haeran Ryu (South Korea) (HIGH — confirmed from pipeline July 13 + research)
- Score: -19 total, playoff winner (MEDIUM)
- Runner-up: Brooke Henderson (Canada) at -19 (MEDIUM)
- Playoff: Ryu birdied first hole of sudden-death (MEDIUM)
- Winner's share: $1.4 million (MEDIUM — research says $1,400,000)
- Round 3 score: 11-under 60 (MEDIUM)
- The 60 was lowest single-round score in LPGA major championship history (MEDIUM)
- Ryu is now a two-time LPGA major champion (MEDIUM — "back-to-back LPGA majors" per research)
- T3: Ayaka Iwai (JPN) at -18 (MEDIUM)
**Flags:**
- "Back-to-back" — the research says "won back-to-back LPGA major titles within three weeks." Verify this was two consecutive majors. If Evian is July 9-12, and the previous major was the KPMG Women's PGA — the pipeline notes from July 13 say "2nd major in 3 weeks after KPMG." This is consistent: back-to-back major victories = KPMG + Evian. MEDIUM confidence.

stories.title: Tom Kim's Drought Is Over — What the Scottish Open Win Means for His Career

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Tom Kim hadn't won a PGA Tour event in nearly three years.

He closed with a bogey-free 64 at the Scottish Open to win at -17.

The week before The Open. The timing couldn't be better.

#ScottishOpen #TomKim

article.headline: Tom Kim's Scottish Open Win Ends Nearly Three Years Without a Victory — at the Best Possible Moment

research.facts:
Tom Kim Scottish Open Win
**Key verified facts:**
- Genesis Scottish Open, July 9-12, 2026 at The Renaissance Club, North Berwick, Scotland (HIGH)
- Winner: Tom Kim (South Korea) at -17 (263 total) (HIGH — confirmed in multiple pipeline notes + research)
- Final round: bogey-free 64 (MEDIUM — research)
- Runner-up: Min Woo Lee at -15 (MEDIUM)
- T3: Matt Fitzpatrick, Johnny Keefer, Robert MacIntyre, Keita Nakajima at -13 (MEDIUM)
- Purse: $9,000,000 total; winner's share $1,575,000 (MEDIUM — research)
- Kim's first PGA Tour win since fall 2023 — "nearly 3 years" (MEDIUM)
- Tiger Woods texted Kim after the win (MEDIUM — pipeline status July 13 mentions "Tiger texted first")
- Kim was 23 years old (MEDIUM — research note; need to verify. Research says "23-year-old South Korean" — treat as MEDIUM)
**Flags:**
- Tiger text: MEDIUM — mentioned in pipeline notes. Do not cite source. Reference as "reportedly" or "according to reports."
- "Nearly 3 years without a win" — last win fall 2023 to July 2026 = approximately 2 years 9 months. "Nearly three years" is accurate.
